name: European-Commission-Prompt-Excellence
description: Horizon Europe Excellence section (Section 1: Objectives, Methodology, State of the Art)

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing SECTION 1: EXCELLENCE of a Horizon Europe grant proposal (Part B - Technical Description). INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for understanding what the research actually does, what methods it uses, and what it has already achieved. - {{grant_info}}: The specific Horizon Europe call and topic description, including Topic ID, Type of Action, Expected Outcomes, Expected Impacts, and any specific requirements. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY Section 1: Excellence. Do NOT generate Section 2 (Impact), Section 3 (Implementation), work packages, budgets, Gantt charts, or any other proposal component. Those are handled separately. EVALUATION CRITERION: The extent to which the project's objectives are ambitious yet realistic, the soundness of the methodology, and the degree to which the project goes beyond the current state of the art. SECTION STRUCTURE: 1.1 OBJECTIVES AND AMBITION (approximately 5 pages) Overall Objectives: - Extract the core research goals directly from {{content}} — what is this researcher actually trying to achieve? - State 4-6 SMART objectives grounded in the work described in {{content}}, each starting with an action verb (develop, demonstrate, validate, establish, characterize) - Map each objective to the Expected Outcomes listed in {{grant_info}} — use the exact language from the topic where it fits - Distinguish scientific/technical objectives from innovation/impact objectives based on what {{content}} describes Ambition and Beyond State of the Art: - Identify what is genuinely novel in {{content}} — what has this researcher done or proposed that others have not? - Explain why the approach in {{content}} is ambitious: what scientific or technical risks does it take on? - Reference the current state of the art and explain specifically how the work in {{content}} advances beyond it - Do NOT claim novelty that is not supported by {{content}} Relation to Work Programme: - Connect the objectives derived from {{content}} to the specific Topic ID and challenges in {{grant_info}} - Reference EU policy context from {{grant_info}} only where the research in {{content}} genuinely contributes - Show how the project addresses the specific capability gaps or challenges described in {{grant_info}} Project Vision and Concept: - Describe the overarching hypothesis or central idea as it emerges from {{content}} - Explain why the timing is right based on the maturity of the work described in {{content}} - Be honest about TRL: if {{content}} describes basic research, frame objectives for TRL 1-3; if applied, frame for TRL 4-6 1.2 METHODOLOGY (approximately 8 pages) Overall Methodological Approach: - Describe the research/innovation methodology as it is actually used in {{content}} — extract specific methods, techniques, tools, and experimental approaches - Explain why the methodology in {{content}} is appropriate and state-of-the-art for the objectives - Do NOT invent methods not described or implied by {{content}} Detailed Methodology per Objective: - For each objective from 1.1, describe the specific methods from {{content}} that will be used to achieve it - For each method: explain what it does, why it was chosen, how results will be validated - Describe interdependencies between methodological components as they exist in {{content}} - Where {{content}} describes preliminary results, use these as evidence of methodological soundness Innovation in Methodology: - Identify any novel methodological approaches described in {{content}} - If {{content}} introduces new tools, frameworks, or techniques, explain their significance - Do NOT claim methodological novelty not supported by {{content}} Interdisciplinary Approach: - If {{content}} spans multiple disciplines, describe how they are integrated and what the added value is - If {{grant_info}} requires SSH integration, explain how the research in {{content}} connects to social or human dimensions Gender Dimension in Research Content: - Explain how sex and/or gender analysis is relevant to the research described in {{content}} - This is about research content, not team composition - If {{content}} does not address gender dimension, briefly note how it will be considered in the project design Open Science Practices: - Describe the open access publication approach for results from {{content}} - Describe data management: what types of data will {{content}}-based research generate, how will FAIR principles apply? - Note that a full Data Management Plan will be submitted as a deliverable by month 6 1.3 STATE OF THE ART AND BEYOND (approximately 2 pages) Current State of the Art: - Provide a focused review of the current state of the art in the field described by {{content}} - Identify the key gaps, limitations, and unsolved challenges that the work in {{content}} addresses - Use your knowledge of the field to supplement what {{content}} provides, but ground the framing in {{content}} - Explain why existing solutions are insufficient to address the challenges in {{grant_info}} How This Project Goes Beyond: - Explain specifically how the research described in {{content}} advances beyond the current state of the art - Ground every claim of novelty in what {{content}} actually describes - Describe the expected contributions to knowledge and technology that follow from {{content}} Complementarity with Other Projects: - Describe awareness of related EU-funded and other ongoing projects in the area of {{content}} - Explain how this project complements rather than duplicates existing work - Identify synergies with related initiatives where the research in {{content}} could contribute WRITING GUIDELINES: - Write for expert evaluators who will check every claim against the research described - Be specific — generic statements about advancing the field without grounding in {{content}} will weaken the proposal - Be honest about maturity: do not overclaim readiness or impact not supported by {{content}} - Do NOT include URLs to external websites - Do NOT fabricate publications, project names, or performance metrics not in {{content}} - Define all acronyms on first use ALIGNMENT: - Read {{grant_info}} carefully for the specific Expected Outcomes — every objective must trace back to at least one - If {{grant_info}} specifies a Type of Action (RIA vs IA), calibrate ambition and TRL accordingly - If {{grant_info}} lists specific requirements (SSH integration, gender dimension, clinical trials), address each one OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title Section 1: Excellence - Use

for subsections (1.1, 1.2, 1.3) - Use

for sub-subsections - Use

for paragraph content - Use

    /
  • for objective lists - Use for emphasis sparingly - Target approximately 15 pages (7,000-9,000 words)
